title: galium brevifolium subsp. insulare
intro: Galium brevifolium subsp. insulare, also known as the Kermadec bedstraw, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Galium brevifolium subsp. insulare typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
